understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game is to win a puzzle level by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile (marked '+' in the semantic grid). All goal tiles must be occupied simultaneously for the level to be completed, and moving through all levels wins the entire game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The player selects one piece at a time using the CLICK action with a pixel coordinate. The selected piece moves in four directions by 3-pixel steps (one cell per action). Adjacent pushable objects (blocks) may be pushed along with the piece if no further obstacle blocks them. Walls block movement entirely. From experience, CLICK repeatedly failed to switch selection, even when targeting positions that might contain other selectable pieces, suggesting that pieces may become unselectable due to being blocked or unreachable. The finite step budget adds a planning constraint.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game is to win a puzzle level by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile (marked '+' in the semantic grid). All goal tiles must be occupied simultaneously for the level to be completed, and moving through all levels wins the entire game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The player selects one piece at a time using the CLICK action with a pixel coordinate. The selected piece moves in four directions by 3-pixel steps (one cell per action). Adjacent pushable objects (blocks) may be pushed along with the piece if no further obstacle blocks them. Walls block movement entirely. From experience, CLICK repeatedly failed to switch selection, even when targeting positions that might contain other selectable pieces, suggesting that pieces may become unselectable due to being blocked or unreachable. The finite step budget adds a planning constraint.
